scope.summary: Constitution rule 16 plus `bounded-run` and `unbounded-wait-lint`: every wait carries a deadline, a timeout is a reportable result, and committed shell cannot reintroduce a wait that never ends.
out_of_scope: The four earlier incidents in the same session, carried by the sibling beads under oraclemcp-eng-program-bp8ia.6.
The rust-oracledb half: its AGENTS.md still carries the twelve-rule constitution and none of these mechanisms.
Waits an agent types ad hoc: the lint covers committed shell only. Rule 16 plus bounded-run is what covers the interactive case.

entry_points.name: scripts/swarm_discipline.sh bounded-run
entry_points.trace: `--selftest` asserts `--timeout 1 -- sleep 30` returns 124 with the report-and-move-on message, and that a fast command is untouched. `--timeout 0` and any value above the 1800s ceiling are usage errors: there is no unbounded mode.

entry_points.name: scripts/swarm_discipline.sh unbounded-wait-lint
entry_points.trace: Over the repository's 96 committed shell files it exits 0. The selftest asserts it exits 65 on `tail -f /var/log/build.log` and 0 on scripts/build_lease.sh, whose `while :;` loop is bounded by a deadline. Both it and the selftest run in the ci.yml lint job as 'swarm discipline (constitution rules 13-17)'.

limitations: The loop heuristic accepts any loop whose body mentions a deadline, timeout, or bounded attempt counter; it proves the shape of a bound, not that the bound is correct.
CI wiring is verified by reading ci.yml and by running both commands locally; no CI run was dispatched from this close.
